sub_sections.title: Objectives and ambition
sub_sections.content: MAESTRO defines eight measurable objectives for HORIZON-CL4-2026-05-DIGITAL-EMERGING-02. OBJ-1: Neuro-symbolic planning engine for autonomous task decomposition — ≥40% improvement in plan success rate over LLM-based baselines (ReAct, Reflexion) on standardised benchmarks (WebArena, SWE-bench, GAIA) for tasks requiring >10 sequential steps (M36, ATU). OBJ-2: Unified adaptive memory architecture — ≥30% improvement in task coherence and factual consistency over context-window-only baselines in sequences exceeding 50 agent steps, measured on MAESTRO's long-horizon evaluation suite (M30, BIIS). OBJ-3: Decentralised multi-agent coordination protocols — coordination of ≥5 heterogeneous agents with ≥25% improvement in joint task completion over independent baselines, and formal proof of protocol convergence (M36, CERIA). OBJ-8: External Tool and API Orchestration Layer — ≥30 external tools/APIs across three demonstrator domains; tool invocation success rate ≥95%; mean recovery time from tool failure ≤5s; provenance traces for 100% of invocations (M36, ATU). OBJ-4: Healthcare demonstrator — clinical decision support — accuracy within 10% of specialist clinician performance on 500 de-identified clinical cases spanning 5 diagnostic domains (M42, NIHS). OBJ-5: Manufacturing demonstrator — process optimisation — ≥15% reduction in defect rates AND ≥10% reduction in energy consumption in pilot factory at ISIA over 3-month operational period (M42, ISIA). OBJ-6: Logistics transfer demonstrator — cross-sector generalisation — ≥20% improvement in disruption recovery time AND ≥15% improvement in delivery schedule adherence across 3 supply chain corridors (M42, ELI). OBJ-7: Open framework release and ecosystem contribution — ≥500 GitHub stars within 6 months of release; contributions to AI-on-demand platform; validation through ≥2 TEFs; technology transfer through ≥3 EDIHs (M48, BAL). Beyond state of the art: MAESTRO co-designs planning, memory, coordination, and tool orchestration as a unified architecture where current research treats LLM-based agents, classical multi-agent systems, and memory-augmented neural architectures as disconnected threads. Formal guarantees — verified sub-task decomposition, provably convergent delegation, auditable traces — distinguish MAESTRO from pure LLM approaches. TRL: starts at TRL 2 (technology concept) targeting TRL 5 (validated in relevant environment) for demonstrators by M42.

sub_sections.title: Methodology
sub_sections.content: The methodology structures nine work packages in three parallel research tracks converging through integration and validation. WP2 (Neuro-Symbolic Planning and Reasoning Engine, ATU lead) delivers OBJ-1's Neuro-symbolic planning engine for autonomous task decomposition through T2-01 (sub-task graph decomposition design with formal pre/post-conditions), T2-02 (MCTS and HTN planning integration with LLM heuristics), and T2-03 (plan monitoring and self-correction implementation), producing D2-01 (Neuro-symbolic planning architecture specification, M18) and D2-02 (Planning engine software prototype, M36). WP3 (Adaptive Memory Architecture, BIIS lead) delivers the Unified adaptive memory architecture through T3-01 (tri-store memory architecture design), T3-02 (memory indexing and retrieval strategy implementation), and T3-03 (forgetting mechanism and coherence maintenance), producing D3-01 (Memory architecture design specification, M12) and D3-02 (Adaptive memory system software, M30). WP4 (Decentralised Multi-Agent Coordination, CERIA lead) develops Decentralised multi-agent coordination protocols through T4-01 (task-delegation and negotiation protocol design), T4-02 (convergence and conflict resolution under partial observability), and T4-03 (auditable multi-agent interaction trace module), producing D4-01 (Multi-agent coordination protocol specification, M18) and D4-02 (Decentralised coordination software, M36). The External Tool and API Orchestration Layer is developed as a cross-cutting capability (OBJ-8) spanning WP2–WP4. WP8 (Integration, Evaluation and Benchmarking, FIIT lead) integrates all pillars via T8-02 and produces D8-01 (Evaluation framework and benchmark specification, M12). The interdisciplinary approach combines AI/ML (ATU, Boreal AI Labs Oy), formal methods (Centre Européen de Recherche en Informatique Avancée), clinical informatics (Nordic Institute of Health Sciences), industrial engineering (Istituto Superiore di Ingegneria Avanzata), logistics operations (EuroLog International AG), and evaluation methodology (Fundación Ibérica de Innovación Tecnológica). SSH: proportionate to the primarily technical research; WP5 includes clinical validation with human experts addressing responsible AI in healthcare. Gender dimension in R&I content: no gender-specific hypotheses apply to agent architecture research; use cases serve diverse end-user populations by design. Open science: open-source release (D9-02, M36), benchmarks shared via AI-on-demand platform, evaluation datasets (D5-02, D7-02, D8-02) FAIR-compliant. Data management plan: D1-02 delivered by M6.

claim_statuses.claim_summary: Eight objectives aligned with call expected outcomes EO-01 and EO-02
OBJ-1 targets: ≥40% plan success improvement, >10 steps, named benchmarks
OBJ-2 targets: ≥30% coherence improvement, 50 agent steps threshold
OBJ-3 targets: ≥5 agents, ≥25% joint task improvement, convergence proof
OBJ-8 targets: ≥30 tools, ≥95% success, ≤5s recovery, 100% traces
Demonstrator KPIs: OBJ-4 (10%, 500 cases, 5 domains), OBJ-5 (≥15%, ≥10%), OBJ-6 (≥20%, ≥15%)
TRL 2 start to TRL 5 end for demonstrators consistent with CC-01
Beyond state of art: unified architecture vs disconnected research threads
WP structure (WP2-WP4 research, WP5-WP7 demonstrators, WP8 integration)
Open science: open-source release, AI-on-demand, TEFs, EDIHs, FAIR data
External Tool and API Orchestration Layer cross-cutting across all pillars
